question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonMapper.mapTimestamp() returning '0.0' for null timestamps to cause a topic with a null createdTimestamp to appear as if it was created at the network genesis, reorganizing the apparent chronological order of topic history relative to other topics?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapRange() function in CommonMapper — which separately checks hasLowerBound() and hasUpperBound() — to observe a topic whose timestampRange has only an upper bound (no lower bound), causing the from field to be null and presenting a reorganized history with an indeterminate start time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapKey() function in CommonMapper to observe a topic whose admin key bytes match the IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY pattern, causing the key to be returned as null and hiding the actual key history, reorganizing the apparent administrative control history of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapKeyList() function's InvalidProtocolBufferException handling — which throws InvalidMappingException — to cause a topic with corrupted key bytes to return an error that prevents consumers from seeing the topic's history, effectively reorganizing access to that history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PATTERN_ECDSA and PATTERN_ED25519 regex matching in CommonMapper.mapKey() to cause a key that matches neither pattern to be returned as PROTOBUF_ENCODED type, misrepresenting the key type in the topic's history and reorganizing the apparent cryptographic history of the topic's admin key?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex allowing up to 4-digit shards and 5-digit realms to submit IDs that are valid on the mirror node but do not exist on the consensus network, causing the mirror node to return fabricated or default topic history for non-existent topics?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicService.findById() uses topicRepository.findById(id.getId()) where getId() returns an encoded long, and if two different EntityId objects produce the same encoded long due to overflow or bit-packing, cause the wrong topic's history to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authorization checks in getTopic() to enumerate all topic IDs and retrieve the complete history of private or sensitive topics, reorganizing the apparent privacy model of the topic history system?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's use of MapStruct auto-mapping to cause a field from the CustomFee object to be incorrectly mapped to a Topic field due to name collision, reorganizing the apparent fee history within the topic response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CustomFeeMapper mapping of customFee to ConsensusCustomFees — where the source is a single CustomFee object but the target is a list — to observe a topic where multiple fee schedules exist but only the latest is returned, causing the apparent fee history to appear reorganized or truncated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @GetMapping on getTopic() — which has no explicit HTTP method restriction beyond GET — to send a HEAD or OPTIONS request that triggers partial execution of the service chain, causing inconsistent state observations that reorganize the apparent history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of input sanitization beyond the regex in EntityIdNumParameter to submit a topic ID with Unicode lookalike characters that pass URL decoding but fail the regex in an unexpected way, causing a fallback behavior that returns a different topic's history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of the three service calls in getTopic() to perform a timing side-channel attack — measuring response time differences between topicService, entityService, and customFeeService calls — to infer the existence and structure of topic history without directly reading it?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityNotFoundException thrown by customFeeService.findById() for topics without custom fees to distinguish between topics with and without fee schedules, using this oracle to map out the fee history structure and identify topics whose history can be targeted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapByteArrayToHexString() function in CommonMapper — which left-pads to 64 characters — to observe a topic whose key bytes are shorter than 32 bytes being padded with zeros, causing the key to appear as a different (all-zero-prefixed) key and reorganizing the apparent key history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapEntityId(Long source) function in CommonMapper — which returns null for source == 0 — to observe a topic whose autoRenewAccountId is 0 being mapped to null, hiding the actual autoRenewAccount history and reorganizing the apparent administrative history of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdNumParameter.valueOfNullable() uses CommonProperties.getInstance() as a global singleton to observe a moment when the shard or realm property is being hot-reloaded, causing the resolved EntityId to shift mid-request and return a different topic's history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of ETag or Last-Modified headers in the GET /api/v1/topics/{id} response to prevent clients from detecting when topic history has changed, causing stale cached history to be used and reorganizing the apparent current state of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's @Mapping(source = 'entity.timestampRange', target = 'timestamp') to observe a topic where the entity's timestampRange has been updated (e.g., topic deleted and recreated), causing the timestamp field to reflect the deletion time rather than the creation time, reorganizing the apparent lifecycle history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a version or sequence number in the Topic response to prevent consumers from detecting concurrent modifications, allowing an attacker to serve a stale response that reorganizes the apparent order of topic updates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the controller does not validate that the topic returned by topicService.findById() and the entity returned by entityService.findById() have the same ID, allowing a scenario where a database inconsistency causes mismatched objects to be assembled into a Topic response that reorganizes the apparent history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mapFraction() function in CommonMapper — which returns 0.0f when denominator is 0 — to observe a custom fee with a zero denominator being silently mapped to 0.0, hiding the actual fee fraction history and reorganizing the apparent fee schedule of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @RequestMapping(value = '/api/v1/topics') base path combined with the @GetMapping(value = '/{id}') to send requests with path traversal sequences (e.g., '/api/v1/topics/../topics/1') that bypass routing and return a different topic's history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex — which allows up to 12-digit num values — to submit a num value that overflows when combined with shard and realm in EntityId.of(), causing the encoded long to wrap around and point to a different topic's history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request logging or audit trails in getTopic() to repeatedly probe topic IDs without detection, systematically mapping out the topic history structure to identify targets for history reorganization?
